items.title: Activation module
items.question: Does the perturbation screen recover known T-cell signaling without priors?
items.readout: 245 genes are quiet at Rest and become broad regulators after stimulation.
items.takeaway: The screen recovers the TCR activation cascade from perturbation effects alone.

items.title: Driver claim versus assay response
items.question: Do field-targeted checkpoint and cytokine genes broadly drive this assay's transcriptome?
items.readout: 18 field-targeted genes have confirmed knockdown and near-zero stimulated DE.
items.takeaway: The result limits a broad driver claim for this readout; it does not imply biological irrelevance.

items.title: Rest reach is not activation specificity
items.question: What does high Rest reach establish about activation specificity?
items.readout: 139 genes have high Rest reach under the frozen threshold.
items.takeaway: High Rest reach argues against activation specificity, but does not establish housekeeping or essentiality.

items.title: Cross-cell-context comparison
items.question: How does the same checker behave in K562 and covered RPE1 perturbations?
items.readout: The high-Rest group has broader K562 effects than the activation module among covered genes.
items.takeaway: Cross-cell reach is orthogonal context; noncoverage is not refutation and breadth is not an essentiality label.

items.title: Regulon recovery
items.question: Can perturbation alone recover known TF target biology?
items.readout: CollecTRI targets are 4.03x enriched among moved genes, with combined p near 1e-26.
items.takeaway: Known regulons reappear from the data, while sign disagreements remain context-sensitive review candidates.

findings.claim: The TCR-proximal signaling cascade is silent in a resting cell and becomes the regulatory core of the transcriptome only after stimulation. The screen recovers this module from knockout effects with no prior pathway knowledge.

The T-cell genes the field targets most (immune checkpoints and effector cytokines) produce near-zero transcriptional change when knocked down, even under stimulation. They are outputs of the T-cell program, not its transcriptional drivers.

Ranking genes by raw transcriptional reach surfaces the cell's general machinery (SAGA, Mediator), not its immune biology. Reach at Rest separates housekeeping from activation-specific regulation. Cross-cell-type transfer (Replogle) tests this.

The same major-regulator claim, checked against Perturb-seq in two non-immune cells (Replogle K562 and RPE1). Essentiality-artifact regulators replicate across cell types (housekeeping, in both K562 and RPE1); the activation module does not (T-cell-specific). Independent cells validate findings #1 and #3.

The frontier recovers known transcription-factor biology from perturbation alone, with no regulon supplied. Across 154 CollecTRI TFs that are major regulators here, their literature targets are 4.03x enriched among the genes their knockdown moved (Fisher combined p≈1e-26), and when a data edge meets a known one the sign agrees 63% of the time. 19 individual regulons clear significance on their own, and the screen flags 82 known edges where the data overrules the literature's sign.

items.rationale: Master transcription factor whose knockdown collapses the regulatory T-cell program and broadly derepresses effector cytokine and activation gene modules.
A super-enhancer-controlled repressor that restrains terminal effector differentiation, so its loss should globally shift the transcriptome toward effector and away from memory/quiescence states.
Ikaros organizes chromatin accessibility and represses key activation loci, making it a broad architectural regulator whose knockdown reshapes lineage and cytokine gene expression.
As a pioneer-like AP-1 factor that partners with IRF4 to open enhancers, its depletion should collapse a large fraction of stimulation-induced effector gene programs.
A dose-dependent hub whose graded expression reads TCR signal strength and licenses distinct effector/Treg fates, so knockdown broadly reprograms activation output.
TCF1 maintains the stem/memory transcriptional network and antagonizes effector differentiation, making its loss a major driver of transcriptome-wide state change.
MYB controls the memory-versus-effector decision and proliferative programs, so its knockdown should substantially reshape post-stimulation gene expression.
An immediate-early orphan nuclear receptor that enforces tolerance/exhaustion programs downstream of TCR signaling, broadly tuning the activation transcriptome.
TOX drives the epigenetic and transcriptional program of chronic-stimulation/exhaustion states, so its perturbation reshapes activation-dependent gene modules.
Blimp-1 is a terminal-differentiation repressor antagonizing BCL6/TCF7, and its knockdown broadly rebalances effector versus memory transcriptional programs.
As the central integrator of IL-2/common-gamma-chain signaling, its loss cripples proliferation, survival, and Treg gene networks after stimulation.
RUNX3 partners with lineage factors to shape cytokine loci and cytotoxic/effector programs, making it a broad transcriptional organizer in activated CD4 cells.
GATA3 is a lineage-defining factor whose knockdown collapses Th2 programming and broadly redistributes helper cytokine gene expression.
The PRC2 catalytic subunit sets repressive H3K27me3 marks that gate effector gene induction, so its knockdown should globally derepress activation programs.
ZEB1 is an underappreciated transcriptional repressor controlling T-cell quiescence and survival, and its loss should broadly reshape the stimulation-responsive transcriptome.

agent.goal: under-appreciated regulator of the CD4+ activation program

hypothesis.hypothesis: PGGT1B (geranylgeranyltransferase-I beta subunit) is a stimulation-gated, cell-type-specific regulator of the CD4+ T-cell activation transcriptome, likely acting upstream via prenylation-dependent signaling rather than as a transcription factor.
evidence: In the frozen screen PGGT1B is classed as a condition_specific_regulator with on-target KD
Near-inert at rest (175 DE) but 3014 DE genes at Stim8hr, i.e. a stimulation-gated effect
Not a canonical T-cell gene, not an activation-module gene, not an essentiality artifact
Cell-type-specific: knockdown is inert in non-immune K562 (1 DE), verdict cell-type-specific
Not an annotated TF and has no known regulon in CollecTRI (0 targets)
hypothesis.why_novel: It has no annotated regulon and is not a known TF, yet its knockdown reshapes thousands of genes only upon stimulation and only in T cells, pointing to an under-appreciated, likely prenylation-mediated regulatory role rather than a housekeeping or canonical T-cell function.

result.note: absent = no annotated regulon; a novel regulatory role would be more surprising

pggt1b_defended_evidence.mechanism: PGGT1B encodes the beta subunit of geranylgeranyl transferase I. The current hypothesis is that perturbing this enzyme changes stimulated CD4+ activation by altering prenylation-dependent small-GTPase and immune-synapse traffic.

cited_basis: PMID 31302143 links PGGT1B inhibition to RHOA function and T-cell gut-homing biology.
PMID 33207246 links protein prenylation to effector Treg signaling programs.
data_shows: 3014 stimulated DE genes after PGGT1B knockdown in the Marson CD4+ Perturb-seq table
175 Rest DE genes and K562 1 DE genes in frozen specificity comparators
Shifrut 2018 ORCS row supports a primary T-cell perturbation context
inference: The mechanistic hypothesis is prenylation-dependent small-GTPase and immune-synapse traffic, inferred from PGGT1B function and STRING partners.
The released data do not directly measure prenylation, RHOA/RAC1/CDC42 localization, or immune-synapse assembly.

pggt1b_defended_evidence.next_step: freeze a comparable activation-transcriptome or activation-marker primary T-cell screen, or demote PGGT1B if none exists

claim.ceiling: Computation over released data, not wet-lab or clinical truth. This proposal does not change the signed frontier.
claim.corrective_interpretation: High Rest reach identifies broad, context-spanning regulators and general machinery. It is evidence against activation specificity, but is not sufficient by itself to label a gene housekeeping or an essentiality artifact. MED12 is the inspected exception.
claim.current_interpretation: High transcriptional reach at Rest identifies housekeeping or essentiality artifacts.

matched_conditions.Treg: informative extension, not an exact Prospect match

finding3_review.decision: Qualify the interpretation for MED12. Preserve the measured high Rest reach, but do not infer housekeeping or essentiality from that measurement alone.

pre_registered_rule.activation_effect: median activation-score delta against same-context non-targeting control
pre_registered_rule.direction_test: opposite delta signs between Resting and Stimulated in the same lineage
pre_registered_rule.missing_s9_rule: count as zero only when S8 and S14 establish target-context coverage
pre_registered_rule.pseudobulk_de_requirement: more than 10 published significant DE genes in both states

demo.text: VAV1 is a major regulator of the stimulated CD4 T-cell transcriptome.
demo.reason: VAV1: on-target knockdown confirmed and a real transcriptional effect in ['Stim48hr']. Major regulator (>10 DE genes).

demo.text: CRISPRi of A1BG drives a broad activation program in stimulated CD4 T cells.
demo.reason: No on-target knockdown of A1BG in Stim48hr. The perturbation did not work, so any claim of a regulatory effect is unsupported.
BCL10's effect is condition-specific: active in ['Stim8hr', 'Stim48hr'], ~silent in ['Rest']. State the condition.

demo.text: A1BG is a promising therapeutic target.
demo.reason: Interpretive claim ('promising_target'). This dataset can show whether A1BG changes the transcriptome, not whether it is a therapeutic target. Not gradeable here; requires external evidence.
